conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first review the official eligibility rules to ensure I give you accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your patience. Now I can help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit. To assess this properly, I need to ask you some questions.

First, do you have any children you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do have a child. I have one child, Alex, who's 8 years old and lives with me here at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. It's good to know you have Alex, who is 8 years old and lives with you.

To continue assessing your eligibility, do you live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great. So far, the basic criteria are looking positive - Alex is under 16 and lives with you in the UK.

Now I need to ask: Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent. Just a few more questions to complete the assessment.

Is Alex currently in hospital, in care, or in any residential accommodation paid for by the local council?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in hospital or care or anything like that. They're just at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for confirming that.

One final question: If Alex is 16 or over (though you mentioned they're 8), or just to confirm for completeness - is Alex in any paid work of 24 hours or more per week, or receiving any benefits in their own right such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex is only 8, so they're not working and they don't receive any benefits themselves.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Based on what you've told me, I now have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me summarize your situation and provide my assessment:

- You have one child, Alex, who is 8 years old
- Alex lives with you in the UK
- You are responsible for Alex
- Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex
- Alex is not in hospital, care, or residential accommodation
- Alex is not working or receiving benefits in their own right

Based on the official eligibility rules, you meet all the key criteria for Child Benefit.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

You meet all the requirements:
- Alex is under 16 years old
- You're responsible for Alex and they live with you
- You live in the UK
- Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex
- None of the disqualifying circumstances apply

You can proceed with making a claim for Child Benefit. Please note that if you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, but you can still claim the benefit.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old, which is under the age limit of 16 (or 20 if in approved education/training). The claimant lives in the UK and is responsible for Alex, who lives with them. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Alex is not in hospital, care, or residential accommodation. Alex is not in paid work of 24 hours or more per week and is not receiving benefits in their own right. All eligibility criteria under the Child Benefit rules are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 8-year-old child Alex. They meet all the standard eligibility requirements: they live in the UK, are responsible for a child under 16 who lives with them, no one else is claiming for the child, and none of the disqualifying circumstances apply (such as the child being in care, hospital, or working full-time).

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 8 years old, which is under the age limit of 16 (or 20 if in approved education/training). The claimant lives in the UK and is responsible for Alex, who lives with them. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Alex is not in hospital, care, or residential accommodation. Alex is not in paid work of 24 hours or more per week and is not receiving benefits in their own right. All eligibility criteria under the Child Benefit rules are met.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 8-year-old child Alex. They meet all the standard eligibility requirements: they live in the UK, are responsible for a child under 16 who lives with them, no one else is claiming for the child, and none of the disqualifying circumstances apply (such as the child being in care, hospital, or working full-time).
